protocol_versions.name: Arkema Protocol w/ media
protocol_versions.description: Imported from NMC Cathode Formulations 2025.xlsx summary sheet.

process_runs.name: N16 cathode parent process
process_runs.notes: Testing of Hx-e (N16a:HCl conc. acid washed and N16b: HCl+Base washed- Eli)

process_runs.name: N15 cathode parent process
process_runs.notes:
Testing of Hx-e (HCl acid washed) from Joe's protocol.
Used for pressing, thickness coating experiment.

process_runs.name: N14 cathode parent process
process_runs.notes:
Large batch preparation for cylindrical/pouch making. Testing of coating thickness vs loading. Increase AM loading to 95%.
Used for pressing, thickness coating experiment.

process_runs.name: N13 cathode parent process
process_runs.notes: Comparison against newly recevied NMC811 from Targray (BASF). Testing w/ Cx(Beta) (acid washed) + CNTs. Compare to N10.

process_runs.name: N12 cathode parent process
process_runs.notes: Repeat of trials with NMC(BASF), attempt to get high quality data. Utilizing Cx(B) acid washed.

process_runs.name: N11 cathode parent process
process_runs.notes: Repeat of trials with NMC(MSE), attempt to get high quality data. Utilizing Cx(B) acid washed.

process_runs.name: N10 cathode parent process
process_runs.notes:
Comparison against newly recevied NMC811 from Targray (BASF). Testing w/ Cx(Beta) (acid washed). Compare to N9/8.
Noticed a big difference in Li chip quality from a fresh package (no dulling). Used fresh lithium chips for testing.

process_runs.name: N9 cathode parent process
process_runs.notes: Comparison against newly recevied NMC811 from Targray (BASF). Testing w/ Cx(echo). Compare to N8

process_runs.name: N8 cathode parent process
process_runs.notes: Comparison against newly recevied NMC811 from Targray (BASF). Utilize Super P for baseline, good for comparison against N5.

process_runs.name: N6 cathode parent process
process_runs.notes: Comparison test against Cx(e). Large batch made for calendaring study investigation.

process_runs.name: N5 cathode parent process
process_runs.notes:
Baseline testing with Super P and MWCNTs.
This test had to be repeated several times due to protocol errors, and Biologic cycler malfunction. The electrode was tested with several difference electrolytes. First set of tests (N5a, N5b, N5c) had mistakenly put discharge first. Additionally, the use of variables post formation with the wrong sign, resulted in charging during a supposed discharge step, resulting in overpotential.

process_runs.name: N4 cathode parent process
process_runs.notes:
Establish baseline performance with Super P and CNTs. Larger batch for ease of mixing/coating.
Note forgot to add MWCNTs. Need a remake. Can be

process_runs.name: N3 cathode parent process
process_runs.notes: Establish baseline performance with commercial materials.

process_runs.name: N2 cathode parent process
process_runs.notes:
Attempt to increase loading to improve areal capacity.
Electrode quality looks good,

process_runs.name: N1 cathode parent process
process_runs.notes:
Begin testing NMC formulations. Testing without removing from the glovebox.
Electrode quality looks good,

Separate N10 child branch
Calendared. The purpose of this experiment was to test the compatibility of the newly received BASF NMC811 with different versions of Hexite. In this case, Hx-e series that was HCl acid washed was tested.
User-confirmed separate child of N10.

Comparison against newly recevied NMC811 from Targray (BASF). Testing w/ Cx(Beta) (acid washed). Compare to N9/8. | Tracking: 1M LiTFSI, 10% FEC
User-confirmed electrolyte override supplied outside the workbook.
Uncalendered. Retest of N10 electrode with another EL.
User-confirmed electrolyte override supplied outside the workbook.

Separate N10 child branch
Uncal, remake of previous N10 test run with different electrolyte. Purpose was primarily to evaluate different ELs, test Hx-e (HCl acid washed) as well as BASF NMC811.
User response was ambiguous; importer currently treats N10e as a separate child of N10 to avoid incorrectly merging it with another branch.

Separate N10 child branch
Uncalendered. larger voltage range. Slow cycling, testing another EL.
User-confirmed separate child of N10.
Repeat of trials with NMC(BASF), attempt to get high quality data. Utilizing Cx(B) acid washed. | Tracking: cal, 1M LiTFSI, 10% FEC
Repeat of trials with NMC(BASF), attempt to get high quality data. Utilizing Cx(B) acid washed. | Tracking: uncal, 1M LiTFSI, 10% FEC

Hx-e (acid washed)
Repeat of initial trials with BASF NMC811. Utilizing HCl acid washed Hx-e and higher overall loading to achieve higher areal capacity.
User-confirmed child of N12 using acid-washed Hx-e at higher loading.

Hx-e (concentrated HCl acid washed)
Experiment used to evaluate concentrated acid washing of Hx-e series for use with NMC cathodes. Compare to N16b and N15 experiments.

Establish a baseline with Super P +CNTs as the conductives, however, forgot to add CNTs. Decided to test with different electrolytes.  (PVDF HSV1810) | Tracking: uncal, 1.2M LiPF6 1:1:1 + 10% FEC

Baseline testing with Super P and MWCNTs. | Tracking: uncal, 1.2M LiFSI 3:7, 2% VC
Baseline testing with Super P and MWCNTs. | Tracking: uncal, 1.2M LiFSI 3:7, 2% VC

Baseline testing with Super P and MWCNTs. This test had to be repeated several times due to protocol errors, and Biologic cycler malfunction. The electrode was tested with several difference electrolytes. First set of tests (N5a, N5b, N5c) had mistakenly put discharge first. Additionally, the use of variables post formation with the wrong sign, resulted in charging during a supposed discharge step, resulting in overpotential. | Tracking: uncal, 2M LiFSI, 0.2M LiDFOB 3:7
N5a came from a series of tests. Testing the effects of different electrolytes on an NMC811. NMC811 from MSE supplies utilized. An error was noted in the protocol causing discharge first instead of charge as well as overpotential after formation.

Baseline testing with Super P and MWCNTs. This test had to be repeated several times due to protocol errors, and Biologic cycler malfunction. The electrode was tested with several difference electrolytes. First set of tests (N5a, N5b, N5c) had mistakenly put discharge first. Additionally, the use of variables post formation with the wrong sign, resulted in charging during a supposed discharge step, resulting in overpotential. | Tracking: uncal, 1.2M LiFSI, EC:DMC 3:7, 2% VC

Baseline testing with Super P and MWCNTs. This test had to be repeated several times due to protocol errors, and Biologic cycler malfunction. The electrode was tested with several difference electrolytes. First set of tests (N5a, N5b, N5c) had mistakenly put discharge first. Additionally, the use of variables post formation with the wrong sign, resulted in charging during a supposed discharge step, resulting in overpotential. | Tracking: uncal, 1M LiFSI, 1:4 FEC:DMC

Baseline testing with Super P and MWCNTs. This test had to be repeated several times due to protocol errors, and Biologic cycler malfunction. The electrode was tested with several difference electrolytes. First set of tests (N5a, N5b, N5c) had mistakenly put discharge first. Additionally, the use of variables post formation with the wrong sign, resulted in charging during a supposed discharge step, resulting in overpotential. | Tracking: uncal, 1M LiPF6 1:1:1, 5% FEC, 2% VC
